_label_note: AU Stage A strict tier reconciled to deterministic strict-mode engine after Codex/GPT-assisted mismatch review; broader model-derived labels retained in ideal_must_detect for gap discovery. Narrow fixes covered AU public switchboard/reception/role-mailbox contact bait, ACT-vs-Act postal-address false positives, negated material-adverse-change trigger prose, and checksum-valid TFN/ABN/ACN positives. Generated placeholder/dummy ABN/ACN/TFN labels were removed from strict must_detect.
_label_tier_note: Current strict tier was realigned after SA/US precision guards; ideal_must_detect preserves removed strict spans for gap review.

_owner_acceptance.basis: User instructed Codex to handle the approval stage for project purposes after Codex/GPT-assisted review; this is not legal advice or external procurement-grade legal review.

ideal_must_detect.matched_text: a contracted network engineer on night shifts at the Newcastle site, born 03/1979, carries device tag RZ-14-88 and is paid to an account ending 4421
purge DOB and partial account references from ticketing systems
restrict employment/health fields to HR platforms

Under the Privacy Act 1988 (Cth), personal information includes information or an opinion about an identified or reasonably identifiable individual; under the Corporations Act 2001 (Cth) and ASIC guidance, MNPI is information not generally available that would be expected to materially affect financial products.
Generic demographics: phrases like “staff aged in their 30s” or “regional NSW personnel” in guidance notes are non‑identifying and acceptable under our privacy threshold.
